originalTitle: Iran warns of ‘eye for an eye’ response if US follows through on Trump’s threats to destroy infrastructure – as it happened
summary: US president vows to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for each strait of Hormuz attack; Iran’s military says it will stop Gulf oil flow and target regional infrastructure if US acts on threats, state tv reports
content: The Houthis ⁠- the Iran-backed group in Yemen, said on Thursday they had carried ⁠out ⁠a military operation targeting two Saudi ⁠oil tankers, identified as ENCELA and ⁠LAYLIA, saying the vessels violated ‌the ‌naval blockade imposed ‌by the group on Monday. The Houthis’ announcement prompted the Saudi-led coalition to declare that it ⁠would respond “firmly”, in a move that threatens to disrupt global ‌energy supplies beyond the Gulf. The Saudi authorities have not immediately confirmed the strike. The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said it had received reports that a tanker had been struck by an unknown projectile and caught fire in the Red Sea, 70 nautical miles from Saudi Arabia’s Al Shuqaiq. At the time of writing there are no reported casualties and the projectile’s origin has not been specified. Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said that Tehran would respond in kind to any attack on its infrastructure, after Donald Trump’s threat to bomb a bridge or power plant for every ship targeted in the strait of Hormuz. “Our defence doctrine is clear: eye for an eye. Any aggression against Iran, including our infrastructure, will compel a powerful and decisive response,” Araghchi said on X. Iran’s state broadcaster also quoted the Iranian military as saying that US threats “will have no result other than the expansion of the war”. It added that if the US acts on the threats, Iran will stop all Gulf oil flow and target oil, gas, electricity and economic infrastructure in the region. Iran’s top negotiator and powerful parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, also warned on Wednesday that if Tehran cannot export its oil, no other country in the region would be able to do so. He wrote on X: “The equation of this war is clear: either all or none! In a region where we do not sell oil, no one will sell oil. If our security is not ensured, no infrastructure will be safe, and the security of the strait is in the absence of American forces. We have repeatedly said that the situation of the strait will not return to pre-war conditions. He also repeated that the strait of Hormuz “will not return to pre-war conditions”, as he has warned several times before. It all came after Donald Trump threatened retribution against Iran in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday. “From this point forward, any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT, including those located next to, or in, the Capital City of Tehran,” the US president said on Truth Social. The threat came after the US conducted its 11th consecutive day of strikes on Iran, including on aircraft hangars and drone storage sites, as the renewed conflict continues to undermine diplomatic efforts to salvage an interim ceasefire deal. Trump also attended the dignified transfer of four US service members killed in the Middle East over the weekend, the third such ceremony the US president has attended since he launched the war in Iran in February. Speaking to reporters on his way to Dover, Delaware, Trump called it “one of the hardest things to do as a president”. His message to grieving families, he said, will simply be “we love you, we love your child”. The Trump administration signed a nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia that could enable the kingdom to enrich its own uranium for nuclear reactors, US energy secretary Chris Wright said. As my colleague Patrick Wintour reported earlier, the timing of the announcement is controversial as the US’s war with Iran is being fought in part to prevent Tehran enriching uranium domestically and make it near-impossible for Iran to build a nuclear weapon. The plan is expected to be submitted to Congress for review, where it could face opposition among US lawmakers who fear a lack of monitoring safeguards to restrict Saudi Arabia’s uranium enrichment. The Trump administration signed a nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday that could enable the kingdom to enrich its own uranium for nuclear reactors, US Energy secretary Chris Wright said in a press release. According to a statement from the Department of Energy, Wright and his Saudi counterpart, Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman, signed “a peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement” and “an accompanying bilateral safeguards agreement.” The question of what those safeguards look like, given the potential for enriched uranium to be used to make nuclear weapons, will be closely scrutinized when the agreement is sent to Congress for review. The two agreements, the press release said, “lay the legal foundation for a decades-long, multi-billion-dollar partnership that advances several priority economic and strategic objectives, including nuclear nonproliferation.” “These agreements reflect our two nations’ shared commitment to strengthening U.S.-Saudi commercial relations, delivering prosperity at home and security to our allies abroad,” Wright said in a statement. “Rest assured, these agreements uphold the highest standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation, while relying on the world’s best nuclear technology and scientists, designed right here in the United States.” As our colleague Patrick Wintour reported earlier: The timing of the announcement is controversial as the US’s war with Iran is being fought in part to prevent Tehran enriching uranium domestically and make it near-impossible for Iran to build a nuclear weapon. The US-Saudi deal is expected to last 30 years and involve US firms such as Westinghouse in developing the programme. It could allow for the building of a uranium enrichment facility in Saudi Arabia after a joint US-Saudi study. The proposal has been in discussion for years. At one point the Biden administration wanted to condition Saudi Arabia’s access to the technology on normalising relations with Israel, something Saudi Arabia is still refusing to do. The plan is
chineseTitle: 伊朗警告称，如果美国兑现特朗普摧毁基础设施的威胁，伊朗将做出“以眼还眼”的反应
chineseSummary: 美国总统誓言每次霍尔木兹海峡袭击都会摧毁一座伊朗桥梁或发电厂；据伊朗国家电视台报道，伊朗军方表示，如果美国对威胁采取行动，伊朗军方将阻止海湾石油流动并瞄准地区基础设施
chineseContent: 伊朗支持的也门胡塞武装周四表示，他们针对两艘沙特油轮“ENCELA”号和“LAYLIA”号进行了军事行动，并称这些船只违反了该组织周一实施的海军封锁。胡塞武装的声明促使沙特领导的联盟宣布将“坚决”回应，此举可能会扰乱海湾地区以外的全球能源供应。沙特当局尚未立即证实此次袭击。英国海事贸易运营部（UKMTO）表示，已收到报告称，一艘油轮在距离沙特阿拉伯Al Shuqaiq 70海里的红海被不明弹丸击中并起火。截至撰写本文时，尚无人员伤亡报告，也未指明弹丸的来源。伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格奇表示，在唐纳德·特朗普威胁要轰炸霍尔木兹海峡每艘目标船只的一座桥梁或发电厂后，德黑兰将对其基础设施的任何攻击做出同样的回应。阿拉奇在X上表示：“我们的防御原则很明确：以眼还眼。对伊朗的任何侵略，包括我们的基础设施，都将迫使伊朗做出强有力和果断的反应。”伊朗国家广播公司还援引伊朗军方的话说，美国的威胁“除了扩大战争之外不会有任何结果”。声明补充说，如果美国对威胁采取行动，伊朗将停止所有海湾石油流动，并瞄准该地区的石油、天然气、电力和经济基础设施。伊朗最高谈判代表、强大的议会议长穆罕默德·巴格尔·加利巴夫周三也警告称，如果德黑兰无法出口石油，该地区其他国家也无法这样做。他在 X 上写道：“这场战争的等式很明确：要么全部，要么全无！在一个地区，
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
